title: Quiz: two users book the last seat
prompt_md: Two users hit 'Book' on the **last available seat** at almost the same instant, and both get a confirmation — the seat is double-sold. What is the root cause?
options: The database server was too slow to respond in time
A race condition: both requests read the seat as 'available' before either one wrote 'booked', so both passed the check
The frontend forgot to disable the button after the first click
There wasn't enough memory to hold both bookings
explanation_md: This is a classic **race condition**. Booking is really read-then-write: check the seat is free, then mark it booked. When two requests overlap, both read 'free' *before* either writes 'booked', so both pass the check and both write — double-selling the seat. The fix is to make 'check it's free AND book it' one indivisible (atomic) step — e.g. `UPDATE ... WHERE seat_id = ? AND booked_by IS NULL` — so only one request can win. It's not about server speed, memory, or the button.
